text.title: Ruby Sorbet Strict Floor with Tapioca RBI Failure Modes
text.summary: ≥3 antipatterns observed in the wild. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Half the files have no `# typed:` sigil; Sorbet silently skips them.
antipattern.root-cause: Adoption was incomplete; team forgot to add sigils on new files.
antipattern.fix: Pre-commit hook rejects new .rb files without sigils; existing files batched to migrate.

antipattern.symptom: Gem upgraded; RBI not regenerated; CI green but runtime errors return.
antipattern.root-cause: No CI step regenerates / checks shims.
antipattern.fix: Add tapioca check-shims to CI; nightly tapioca update job.

antipattern.symptom: Codebase peppered with T.unsafe(...) calls bypassing types.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents used T.unsafe to make Sorbet shut up.
antipattern.fix: Require justification comment; review board on PRs adding T.unsafe.
